name: Entebbe
summary: Entebbe, Uganda's gateway city and airport hub, is generally safe for tourists with moderate precautions needed. The city benefits from enhanced security due to its proximity to the international airport and government facilities, though petty crime and scams targeting tourists remain concerns.
quickVerdict: Entebbe is relatively safe for tourists, particularly compared to Kampala, with most visitors experiencing trouble-free stays. As Uganda's main entry point, it has good infrastructure and security presence, but travelers should remain vigilant against petty crime and tourist scams. The city serves as an excellent base for exploring Uganda while offering a more relaxed atmosphere than the capital.

neighborhoods.name: Airport Area
neighborhoods.description: Well-secured area around Entebbe International Airport with heavy police presence and good infrastructure. Hotels and businesses here cater to international travelers with enhanced security measures.

neighborhoods.name: Town Center
neighborhoods.description: The main commercial district with shops, restaurants, and markets. Generally safe during daytime with good foot traffic, though visitors should watch for pickpocketing in busy areas.

neighborhoods.name: Entebbe Peninsula
neighborhoods.description: Residential and tourist areas along Lake Victoria including botanical gardens. Safe during day but exercise caution at night, especially in isolated areas near the water.

neighborhoods.name: Government Area
neighborhoods.description: Home to State House and other government facilities with heavy security presence. Very safe but restricted access to some areas.

neighborhoods.name: Kitoro/Nakiwogo
neighborhoods.description: Local residential areas and fishing communities. Generally safe but requires more caution, especially for solo travelers. Limited infrastructure and lighting at night.

neighborhoods.name: Outskirts/Rural Areas
neighborhoods.description: Remote areas outside main town with poor lighting and limited security. Avoid walking alone after dark and use reliable transportation.

scams.name: Airport Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unauthorized taxi drivers at the airport quote inflated prices to tourists, sometimes 3-5 times the normal rate.
scams.howToAvoid: Use only licensed airport taxi services, agree on prices beforehand, or book transfers through your hotel.

scams.name: Fake Tour Operators
scams.description: Individuals pose as legitimate tour guides or operators, collecting deposits for gorilla permits or safaris that don't exist.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book through registered tour operators, verify credentials with Uganda Tourism Board, and avoid paying large sums upfront.

scams.name: ATM Card Skimming
scams.description: Criminals offer help at ATMs to scan your card and observe PIN entry, later draining accounts.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks or hotels, cover PIN entry, and decline help from strangers.

scams.name: Romance/Dating App Scams
scams.description: Scammers use dating apps to lure tourists to expensive bars where they face inflated bills and pressure to pay.
scams.howToAvoid: Suggest meeting locations yourself, check menu prices upon arrival, and be wary of overly forward approaches.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travelers generally report positive experiences in Entebbe with proper precautions. The city is considered safer than Kampala with lower harassment levels, though cultural awareness is important. Most locals are helpful and respectful, but modest dress and avoiding isolated areas at night are recommended.
tips: Dress modestly, covering shoulders and knees, especially outside tourist areas
Book accommodation in well-reviewed hotels with good security
Avoid walking alone after dark and use trusted taxi services
Stay in groups when possible and trust your instincts
Keep emergency contacts and embassy information readily available

nightSafety.overview: Nighttime safety in Entebbe requires moderate caution. While violent crime is less common than in larger cities, petty crime increases after dark. Most areas have limited lighting outside the main commercial zones, and police patrols are less frequent in residential areas.
tips: Use reliable taxi services or ride-sharing apps rather than walking
Stay in well-lit areas with good foot traffic when out at night
Avoid isolated areas near Lake Victoria shores after dark
Travel in groups when possible and inform someone of your plans
Keep valuables secure and avoid displaying expensive items

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Entebbe.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Bolt operate in Entebbe with good coverage, especially between the airport and town center. Both are generally safe and reliable options for tourists.
transport.taxis: Licensed airport taxis are the safest option for airport transfers. Local taxis (matatus) are cheap but often overcrowded and may have safety issues. Private hire taxis are recommended for tourists.
transport.tips: Book airport transfers in advance through reputable companies. Avoid public minibuses (matatus) which can be unsafe. Use motorcycle taxis (boda-bodas) with extreme caution as accidents are common.

customs: Greetings are very important - always shake hands and exchange pleasantries before business
Use your right hand for eating, passing items, and handshakes - the left hand is considered unclean
Show respect to elders by greeting them first and using formal language

health.overview: Entebbe has better healthcare facilities than rural areas, with several private clinics serving expatriates and tourists. Malaria is a major risk requiring prophylaxis.
health.water: Tap water is not safe to drink. Use bottled, boiled, or properly treated water for drinking and brushing teeth.
health.vaccinations: Yellow fever vaccination is mandatory for entry. Recommended vaccines include hepatitis A/B, typhoid, meningitis, and malaria prophylaxis.
health.altitude: Entebbe is at low altitude (1,155m) so altitude sickness is not a concern. Climate is tropical with high humidity year-round.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Kampala: +256-414-306-001, located at 1577 Ggaba Road, Kampala (40km from Entebbe)

faq.a: Yes, Entebbe is generally safe for tourists with basic precautions. It's considered safer than Kampala due to enhanced security around the airport and government facilities. Most visitors experience no serious problems.
Nighttime requires moderate caution in Entebbe. While violent crime is uncommon, petty theft increases after dark. Use reliable transport, stay in well-lit areas, and avoid walking alone at night.

faq.q: Is Entebbe safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travelers generally report positive experiences in Entebbe with proper precautions. Dress modestly, stay in reputable accommodation, and avoid isolated areas at night. Most locals are respectful and helpful.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Entebbe?
faq.a: Avoid remote areas outside the main town, especially after dark. Exercise caution in fishing communities like Nakiwogo at night, and stay away from isolated areas along Lake Victoria shores when alone.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Entebbe?
faq.a: Licensed airport taxis and ride-sharing apps like Uber/Bolt are safe. Avoid overcrowded public minibuses (matatus) and use motorcycle taxis (boda-bodas) with extreme caution due to accident risks.
